title: [Bug]: can't push images to local registry
body:
### Issue Description

Hello guys,

I have a Docker environment on a MacOS, and among other things, I have a local Registry, with http (insecure) access, which I managed to get working with a lot of effort. Now I'm trying to migrate things to work with Podman and right now when trying to push an image I get this error.
`Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:768661b0a155f63fa8ee46919bb77f90d33cc15ae9e2dd0ea4e9abbe8665940c at destination: pinging container registry registry.local:5000: Get "https://registry.local:5000/v2/": dial tcp: lookup registry.local: Temporary failure in name resolution`

The name `registry.local` is defined in `/etc/hosts`. Apparently Podman tries to access the server, but it cannot resolve the name and the URL looks like this, as shown in the log below:
`DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: POST URI: http://d/v4.3.1/libpod/images/registry.local:5000/node-web-app/push
`

command:
`podman --log-level=debug push registry.local:5000/node-web-app`

output:
```
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called push.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level=debug push registry.local:5000/node-web-app)
DEBU[0000] SSH Ident Key "/Users/rlucas/.ssh/podman-machine-default" SHA256:MbhME2ZhzP+DmKSlpbm+yGY+j6FC3k4BDr9WxVFF/t0 ssh-ed25519
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v4.3.1/libpod/_ping
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/Users/rlucas/.config/containers/registries.conf"
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for rpi4-1:5000 in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /Users/rlucas/.config/containers/auth.json
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for 192.168.1.4:5000 in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /Users/rlucas/.config/containers/auth.json
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for localhost:5000 in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /Users/rlucas/.config/containers/auth.json
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for registry.local:5000 in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /Users/rlucas/.config/containers/auth.json
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: POST URI: http://d/v4.3.1/libpod/images/registry.local:5000/node-web-app/push
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:4197f7214e0170f09df9df2a97fe018f41e4e983a31d5e1cc43664d6c7f7bb08
Copying blob sha256:e2c36140d36a57a8031f9bc31159e83c03ff2ec58433f27f3581fd19a689f2c0
Copying blob sha256:3b7c989449c582820a3b7950661c2df29c92939e831c1399c07e818a2d562552

Copying blob sha256:5767e262c35e18f3a0066028f3e678cb40b04cba6ad087b7fbb7ebbe2d2986f6
Copying blob sha256:768661b0a155f63fa8ee46919bb77f90d33cc15ae9e2dd0ea4e9abbe8665940c
Copying blob sha256:a205a7bbdfe04b552fa353d43847917b308780146718f45ecc2a0e499056f0b4
Copying blob sha256:567d43b0f38b2720b36e4fd8760cbf7b2074431e6e59398463033390c5a103cc
Copying blob sha256:ea91e8fcfcc8aca0218a4b5eb0034841d810fe5d2fd5a147008c1d3464f1dc8e
Copying blob sha256:d8fa520091e2e20ff19c51be2291c31f12368e8339a0b4aa114bfd1650ad957a
Copying blob sha256:5727316e11f1ffa1107c57fc648d5276580144eb1924458ee03f8a9dbf43cc89
Copying blob sha256:2cd8016c912b482b37b702776a5d00953a19966893268f7c2d75b234d1d3306b
Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:768661b0a155f63fa8ee46919bb77f90d33cc15ae9e2dd0ea4e9abbe8665940c at destination: pinging container registry registry.local:5000: Get "https://registry.local:5000/v2/": dial tcp: lookup registry.local: Temporary failure in name resolution
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Added to $HOME/.config/containers/registries.conf the registry where I intend to pull the image
```
unqualified-search-registries = ["registry.local:5000"]

[[registry]]
insecure = true
location = "registry.local:5000"
```

2. push the image: `podman --log-level=debug push registry.local:5000/node-web-app`
`Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:768661b0a155f63fa8ee46919bb77f90d33cc15ae9e2dd0ea4e9abbe8665940c at destination: pinging container registry registry.local:5000: Get "https://registry.local:5000/v2/": dial tcp: lookup registry.local: Temporary failure in name resolution`

### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Managed to upload an image to a local registry, connecting via http, in insecure mode.

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

MacOs, Ventura, Intel.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: The main podman code runs in the podman machine VM (linux), so I assume you would need to add the entry there to /etc/hosts. You can use `podman machine ssh` to access it

Hello, thanks for the quick response. I added the entry in hosts, but it still didn't work. I made a video showing step by step. Take a look, please.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/527641/217292765-59529306-b967-489a-bd05-da4bf81eedcd.mp4

Before, I was trying to use podman on both sides: server and client. Now I'm trying to simplify and/or isolate the problem, so on the server side (Linux) I'm using docker with a running docker registry server. Left side of the video.

On the client side, right side of the video, using docker and podman.


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/527641/217299575-f5db3914-8832-4a1f-9258-64d7ca6d47fa.mp4

> Before, I was trying to use podman on both sides: server and client. Now I'm trying to simplify and/or isolate the problem, so on the server side (Linux) I'm using docker with a running docker registry server. Left side of the video.
> 
> On the client side, right side of the video, using docker and podman.
> 
>  podman-2.mp4

@Luap99,
I got resolver by tweaking the configuration in the Podman vm to:
/etc/containers/registries.conf
```
[[registry]]
location="registry.local:5000"
insecure=true
```

Your tip about the VM helped me. Completely forgot about this fact. Thanks.

As a suggested improvement, `Podman Desktop` could allow inserting insecure registers as well and apply the settings on the VM automatically, include `/etc/hosts` configs 😁
That way, we wouldn't even remember that there is a VM behind the scenes 😅
I would suggest this is something potentially podman machine init could handle.
